import re

def detect_malicious_url_scheme(url_string: str) -> bool:
    """
    Determine whether a given string contains a risky URL scheme.
    
    Args:
        url_string: str, the input string to be inspected for potentially malicious URL schemes
        
    Returns:
        bool, returns True if the string contains any dangerous URL schemes, 
        ignoring valid image data URLs; otherwise, False
    """
    # List of dangerous URL schemes to check for
    dangerous_schemes = [
        'javascript:', 'vbscript:', 'vbs:', 'mocha:', 'livescript:', 
        'ecmascript:', 'jscript:', 'about:', 'chrome:', 'opera:', 
        'res:', 'resource:', 'shell:', 'hcp:', 'ms-help:', 'diskmgmt.msc:', 
        'mmc:', 'mk:', 'mhtml:', 'help:', 'disk:', 'afp:', 'x-res:', 
        'ieframe.dll:', 'c:', 'file:', 'outlook:', 'shell.explorer:'
    ]
    
    # Convert to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison
    url_lower = url_string.lower().strip()
    
    # Check for data URLs with image mime types (these are considered safe)
    # Valid image data URLs start with data:image/
    image_data_url_pattern = r'^data:image/(png|jpg|jpeg|gif|bmp|webp|svg\+xml|x-icon|vnd\.microsoft\.icon)'
    if re.match(image_data_url_pattern, url_lower):
        return False
    
    # Check if the URL starts with any dangerous scheme
    for scheme in dangerous_schemes:
        if url_lower.startswith(scheme):
            return True
    
    # Check for data URLs that are NOT images (these are considered dangerous)
    # This catches data: URLs that don't have image mime types
    if url_lower.startswith('data:') and not re.match(image_data_url_pattern, url_lower):
        return True
    
    # If no dangerous schemes found, return False
    return False
